title: Executive Order 12318—Statistical Policy Functions

flags.justification: This consolidates statistical-policy functions into OMB as specifically required by the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1980, implementing Congress's own statutory design rather than an independent executive concentration.
Sec. 4(b) explicitly preserves the requirement for periodic reports to the Committees of Congress.
Multiple related technical amendments to several prior orders all coherently implement one statutorily-mandated reorganization.
An internal reorganization order, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing reorganization of an ongoing government function.
This preserves congressional reporting requirements and implements a congressionally-mandated restructuring under the Paperwork Reduction Act.
An internal reorganization with no third-party compliance regime.

This is part of the foundational establishment of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA) within OMB, an institution that remains enormously influential in federal regulatory review decades later.
A careful, coordinated transfer from Commerce to OMB, implementing a specific statutory mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. Part of the foundational statutory establishment of OIRA, one of the most consequential and enduring institutions in the modern federal regulatory process, yet scoring low due to its congressionally-mandated design and preserved reporting requirements.
